description_long: This bollard is a decorative stone structure with a cylindrical central shaft mounted on a square stone base. The midsection features ornate carvings resembling stylized clouds or floral motifs, adding an artistic touch. The upper portion is capped with a wide, gently sloping roof-like element, topped by a faceted, lotus-bud-shaped finial. The surface shows signs of weathering, with patches of moss or lichen, suggesting outdoor placement. The overall construction appears robust and heavy, typical of traditional stone barriers found in parks, temple grounds, or garden paths. Its design is both functional as a barrier and ornamental, blending utility with aesthetic appeal.
description: A decorative stone bollard with ornate carvings, a wide cap, and a lotus-bud finial, mounted on a square base. Weathered surface and robust construction suggest outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate carvings and the full profile of the lotus-bud finial atop the wide cap.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curvature of the cap and the detailed carvings on the midsection.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carvings and the cylindrical shaft, with less detail visible on the finial.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the weathered texture and the layered structure of the cap and base.
